cli.discovery: Read cliPath, profileRoot and versionCommand from local configuration; the adapter never reads or migrates authentication data.

mappings.provider_semantics: Use Build in a disposable worktree. This is isolated-project work, not a filesystem-readonly promise.
Use Build in a disposable worktree solely to run declared deterministic checks. MiMo does not promise a filesystem-readonly tool mode, so Codex verifies the worktree diff and rejects edits.
Use Build only in the disposable worker worktree for an approved code-change packet.

lifecycle.launch: invoke-codex-praetor.ps1 starts MiMo only after the tuple and isolated worktree contract are approved.
lifecycle.parse_terminal_state: watch-codex-praetor-job.ps1 treats risk-control and semantic failures as terminal evidence, never as success.
lifecycle.cleanup: The job record and worktree retention are controlled by Codex Praetor; no provider database is modified.

evidence.end_to_end: A current-generation provider canary in an isolated worktree is required before real dispatch.
